heading: Prohibition of coercion
label: 6385 Prohibition of coercion
notes: Effective DateSection effective 6 months after , see , set out as a note under , Labor.Feb. 5, 1993section 405(b)(1) of Pub. L. 103–3section 2601 of Title 29

children.label: a
children.text: An employee shall not directly or indirectly intimidate, threaten, or coerce, or attempt to intimidate, threaten, or coerce, any other employee for the purpose of interfering with the exercise of any rights which such other employee may have under this subchapter.

the term “intimidate, threaten, or coerce” includes promising to confer or conferring any benefit (such as appointment, promotion, or compensation), or taking or threatening to take any reprisal (such as deprivation of appointment, promotion, or compensation); and
the term “employee” means any “employee”, as defined by section 2105.
